mission.title: flux: FAQ needs updating to reflect fluxcd.io/ignore annotation
mission.description: FAQ needs updating to reflect fluxcd.io/ignore annotation. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify flux feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flux deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=flux
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: FAQ needs updating to reflect fluxcd.io/ignore annotation

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
In the Flux v1 documentation, the FAQ documents the fluxcd.io/ignore annotation and how it can be used to ignore particular manifests (https://docs.fluxcd.io/en/1.21.0/faq/#can-i-temporarily-make-flux

steps.title: Apply the fix for FAQ needs updating to reflect fluxcd.io/ignore annotation
steps.description:
Introduce `kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io/reconcile` annotation. When set to `disabled`, the controller will no longer apply changes from source, nor it will prune the annotated resource.

Ref: https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/issues/614

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm FAQ needs updating to reflect fluxcd.io/ignore ann is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Introduce `kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io/reconcile` annotation. When set to `disabled`, the controller will no longer apply changes from source, nor it will prune the annotated resource.

Ref: https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/issues/614. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
